title: Interface Routing Framework (IRF): Minimal Operational Protocol for Pipeline-Ready Legal Event-Flow Encoding and IMR Readout (Version 1.0)
author: Huang, Jim Yongzhi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This document introduces the Interface Routing Framework (IRF) as a minimal operational protocol for encoding cross-domain legal event-flows into pipeline-ready, comparable records. The protocol operates at the interface level and defines a fixed representation scheme that enables observable event-flows to be tracked, transformed, and recorded across multiple legal interfaces without semantic unification. 
 IRF is designed as an executable interface layer that can be directly integrated into data pipelines. It standardizes how event-flows are encoded into fixed interface-level records and produces a batchable Institutional Mismatch Rate (IMR) readout for cross-case comparison. The protocol does not perform legal reasoning or doctrinal interpretation; instead, it provides a fixed operational layer for consistent encoding and comparison of institutional processes. 
 This document is intended for integration into computational law, policy analysis, and AI governance workflows where reproducibility, comparability, and structured routing across legal interfaces are required.
